verdict_reason: The traditional reading is a structural-causal chain: attachment to bhoga-aiśvarya captures cetas; cetas-capture blocks samādhi (collected/calmed mind); without samādhi, vyavasāyātmikā buddhi cannot establish. The engineering layer maps this to: status-attachment captures attention; attention-capture blocks the focused state; without focus, resolved engineering judgment cannot establish. Reverse-translation: 'an operator whose attention has been captured by promotion concerns and cannot focus on the substrate' translates back to apahṛta-cetas + bhoga-aiśvarya-prasakti + samādhau na vidhīyate. No distortion. The crucial nuance — this is structural-causal, not moral — is preserved in both directions ('It is not coaching humility'). PASS for both 3.6 and 7.10.
